title.en: The Art of Polite Bargaining in Korea
title.ko: 조금만 깎아 주시면 안 돼요? (물건 살 때 흥정하기)

description.en: It's not about what you say, but how you say it! Master the polite way to ask for a discount using honorifics like "깎아 주시면 안 돼요?"
description.ko: 시장에서 물건을 살 때, 무조건 깎아달라고 떼쓰는 게 아니라 상대를 존중하며 정중하게 가격을 제안하는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Shopping. Market. Negotiation. Manners. Honorifics. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I was at Namdaemun Market today and noticed something interesting. Most people were using the phrase "깎아주세요" to get a discount.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I know the "-yo" at the end makes it polite, but for some reason, it still felt a bit direct. Is there a way to sound more respectful?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 역시 귀가 정말 예민하네요! 맞아요. "요" 를 붙이는 것만으로도 충분히 예의 바르지만 가끔은 딱딱하게 들릴 수 있죠.
content.translation: As expected, John, your ears are really sharp! Right, "-yo" alone is polite enough, but sometimes it can sound stiff.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 단어 중간에..., '-시-' 라는 마법의 양념을 넣어보세요.
content.translation: In those cases, try putting the magic seasoning '-si-' in the middle of the word.

content.speaker: John
content.text: '-시-'? Is that like a "Polite Buffer" inside the verb?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정확해요! 상대방의 행동을 높여주는 표현인데, 영어의 "Would you be so kind as to..." 처럼 문장에 격조를 더해줘요.
content.translation: Exactly! It's an expression that elevates the other person's action, adding class to the sentence like "Would you be so kind as to..." in English.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 상인분들의 기분도 좋아지게 만드는 흥정의 리액션을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn 'high-class bargaining' reactions that also make the merchants feel good!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 조금만 깎아 주시면 안 돼요?

content.speaker: John
content.text: So by adding "시" in "주시면," I'm showing extra respect to the seller, right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 그냥 "깎아주세요" 보다 훨씬 부드럽고 상대를 존중하는 느낌이 들거든요.
content.translation: Right! It feels much softer and more respectful of the other person than just saying "Please cut the price."

insight.title: 조금만 깎아 주시면 안 돼요?
insight.description: 'Can't you give me a little discount?'
insight.usage_tip: '깎다(To cut/discount)' + '주시다(Honorific give)' + '안 돼요(Can't it be/Polite)'. The honorific '-시-' shows that you acknowledge the seller's authority over the price.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 사장님. 이거 조금만 깎아 주시면 안 돼요?
content.translation: Boss, could you give me a little discount on this?

content.speaker: John
content.text: And what if I want to keep the mood bright and friendly?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 때는..., "아이~ 사장님. 조금만 깎아줘요~" 라고 웃으면서 말해 보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try saying it with a smile, "아이~ 사장님, 조금만 깎아줘요~".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이건 격식보다는 친근함으로 사장님의 마음을 녹이는 리액션이에요.
content.translation: This is a reaction that melts the boss's heart with friendliness rather than formality.

insight.title: 조금만 깎아줘요~
insight.usage_tip: A more casual, friendly, and 'Aegyo' version. Use this when you've already had a bit of friendly conversation with the seller.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm at a clothing market and I found a jacket that's slightly over my budget.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 총각. 그 옷 진짜 잘 어울리네! 원래 오만 원인데 내가 특별히 사만 오천 원에 줄게.
content.translation: Young man, that suit looks great on you! It's originally 50,000 won, but I'll specially give it to you for 45,000.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 옷이 정말 예쁘긴 하네요. 마음에 쏙 들어요!
content.translation: The clothes are really pretty. I love them!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그런데 사장님. 조금만 더 깎아 주시면 안 돼요?
content.translation: But Boss, could you give me just a little more discount?

insight.title: 깎아 주시면 안 돼요?
insight.usage_tip: By using '-si-' in '주시면', John sounds like a well-mannered customer who knows the culture.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아이고..., 이것도 정말 많이 깎아준 건데..., 남는 게 없어!
content.translation: Oh dear, I already discounted it a lot... I won't have anything left!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 에이~ 사장님 인심 좋으시잖아요! 기분 좋게 사만 원에 해주세요!
content.translation: Eh~ Boss, you're generous! Please do it for 40,000 won to make us both feel good!

insight.title: 인심 좋으시잖아요!
insight.usage_tip: Acknowledging their generosity makes it harder for them to say no.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아이구. 말을 참 예쁘게 하네! 알았어. 오늘만 특별히 사만 원에 가져가!
content.translation: Haha, you speak so beautifully! Okay, take it for 40,000 won just for today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow! It’s all about the nuance. Adding that little "시" buffer really changed the vibe of the conversation.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국어는 단어 하나보다 그 속에 담긴 존중이 대화의 결과를 바꾼답니다.
content.translation: Right. In Korean, it's the 'respect' inside rather than just a single word that changes the outcome of the conversation.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "polite bargaining" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 조금만 깎아 주시면 안 돼요?
content.translation: Can't you give me a little discount?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아이~ 사장님. 조금만 깎아줘요!
content.translation: Aw, Boss, give me a little discount!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 사장님 인심 좋으시잖아요!
content.translation: Boss, you're famous for your generosity!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 서비스로 하나만 더 주시면 안 돼요?
content.translation: Can't you give me one more as a "service"?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 다음에 또 올게요!
content.translation: I'll come again next time!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 시장에서 물건을 살 때 사장님을 존중하는 따뜻한 말투로 말해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, when you buy something at a market today, try speaking in a warm tone that respects the boss.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "조금만 깎아 주시면 안 돼요?"
content.translation: Can't you give me a little discount?

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 가격이 생각보다 비싸서 조금 깎아달라고 했어요. (The price was higher than I thought, so I asked to cut it a bit.)
품격 있는 말투는 대화에 격조를 더해줘요. (A dignified tone adds class to a conversation.)
시장 사장님들의 넉넉한 인심 덕분에 기분이 좋았어요. (I felt good thanks to the generous kindheartedness of the market bosses.)
'-시-'는 부탁을 할 때 마음을 부드럽게 해주는 완충제 역할을 해요. ('-si-' acts as a buffer that softens the heart when making a request.)
대화할 때는 항상 상대방을 존중하는 태도를 가져야 해요. (You should always have an attitude of respecting the other person when talking.)
물건을 싸게 사는 것보다 흥정하는 과정이 더 재미있어요. (The process of bargaining is more fun than buying things cheaply.)
